New Zealand: The 1995 Act prohibits names that cause offense, are too long, or resemble official titles. Examples of rejected names are Adolf Hitler and Yeah Detroit. Denmark: If parents want a name not on the 7,000 preapproved list, they must get permission from local church and government officials. 15-20% of the 1,100 reviewed names are rejected each year.
